历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for science (physics category) was approximately 13637 → 12953 → 11528 → 10697 → 16173. From 2021 to 2024, the overall trend was moving forward (from 13637 to 10697), but in 2025, due to the reform of the college entrance examination model (changing from arts/science division to '3+1+2' subject selection model), there was a significant backward shift, dropping sharply from 10697 to 16173, a single-year backward shift of about 5476, with drastic fluctuations. The baseline ranking for 2025 is approximately 16173. The university's enrollment plan for science (physics category) in Yunnan Province was 58 students from 2021 to 2023, increased to 60 in 2024, and adjusted back to 57 in 2025. The 2026 plan is 58 students, a slight increase of 1 student (+1.75%) compared to 2025, with a minimal expansion that has limited impact on the competitive landscape.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: 211 Project, Double First-Class construction university; in the 2026 Soft Science China University Rankings, it is approximately 113th, with recent rankings fluctuating between 99 and 113, belonging to the upper-middle tier of 211 universities, with stable overall level. The university has not experienced significant negative public opinion incidents in reputation monitoring. It ranks 50th in Douyin comprehensive popularity, rated as 'relatively hot', with moderate short video exposure and average application attention. No records of enrollment gaps or anomalies. In the fifth round of discipline evaluation, the university did not have disciplines rated A- or above (data not included or did not reach A-), with advantages mainly in characteristic fields such as transportation engineering and navigation technology, and discipline competitiveness at a medium level among 211 universities.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Science and engineering majors such as computer science, electronic information/communication, and electrical engineering continue to have high social popularity (in 2024, electronic information/communication was 'highly recommended', and electrical engineering was 'highly recommended'), which highly matches the university's enrollment majors (computer science, electronic information, automation, etc.), potentially attracting more high-scoring candidates. The total number of candidates for the 2026 Yunnan Province college entrance examination is approximately 377,900, a significant increase of about 32% compared to 2025 (approximately 285,700), leading to a substantial expansion in candidate scale and intensified competition. The university is located in Dalian City, Liaoning Province, and recruits across provinces.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the baseline of the significant backward shift in rankings in 2025, the minimal increase of only 1 student in the 2026 enrollment plan, the intensified competition due to a substantial 32% increase in the total number of candidates in Yunnan Province, and the continued high popularity of hot majors like computer science and electronic information attracting high-scoring candidates, it is expected that the minimum admission ranking for science (physics category) in 2026 will continue to shift slightly backward from the 2025 ranking of 16173. The predicted minimum ranking is approximately 16393, with a range of 15893 to 16893.

For rankings significantly better than the lower limit of the range (e.g., within 15800), focus on reaching for Tier 1 popular engineering majors.

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for liberal arts (history category) was approximately 2557 → 2268 → 3560 → 2162 → 2496, showing significant fluctuations. In 2023, due to a sharp drop in the provincial control line (liberal arts first batch from 575 to 530), the ranking peaked at 3560. In 2024, it recovered to 2162, and in 2025, it slightly rose to 2496. Over the past three years, the ranking fluctuated between 2162 and 3560, with a baseline of approximately 2496 in 2025. The enrollment plan for liberal arts (history category) in Yunnan Province was 10 students from 2021 to 2023, reduced to 8 students in 2024–2025. In 2026, the plan is to restore to 10 students, an increase of 2 students (+25%) compared to 2025, which is a relatively large expansion that helps alleviate competition pressure.

学校层次与声誉.title: School Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: A 211 Project and Double First-Class construction university. In the 2026 Soft Science China University Rankings, it is approximately 113th, with recent rankings stable in the 99–113 range, belonging to mid-tier 211 universities with a relatively high level of recognition. No significant negative public opinion incidents have been detected in reputation monitoring. Its comprehensive Douyin popularity ranking is 50th, rated as 'relatively hot', with moderate application attention. No records of enrollment gaps or anomalies have been observed. In the fifth round of discipline evaluation, the university did not have any disciplines rated A- or above.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: The law major has a relatively high social popularity (in 2024, law was rated as 'differentiated wait-and-see', but overall it remains a popular direction in liberal arts), matching the university's law enrollment major. The economics category has a differentiated wait-and-see popularity. The public management category has average popularity. The total number of candidates for the 2026 Yunnan Province college entrance examination is approximately 377,900, a significant increase of about 32% compared to 2025 (approximately 285,700), leading to a substantial expansion in the candidate pool and intensified competition. The university is located in Dalian City, Liaoning Province, and involves cross-provincial enrollment.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the baseline ranking of 2496 in 2025, the mitigating effect of a 25% enrollment expansion in 2026 (from 8 to 10 students) on competition, and the intensified competition due to a roughly 32% increase in the total number of candidates in Yunnan Province, the expansion and candidate growth partially offset each other. The continued popularity of the law major may attract more high-scoring liberal arts candidates, but the overall liberal arts competition landscape remains relatively stable. The predicted minimum ranking is approximately 2890, with a range of 2390–3390.
